summary: Full frontend-only Pom Jar app tested via Playwright against the public URL. All 14 requested scenarios passed including add-task destinations (today/drawer/both), completion incrementing pom count with a colored pom span appearing in [data-testid=jar-body], sort order (category then alpha), edit/save-to-drawer/delete, drawer collapse toggle, drawer add-to-today & delete, localStorage persistence across reload (tasks, drawer, poms, reward), stratified layering across categories, and the 400-pom celebration flow (seeded 399 poms + reward -> completing the 400th triggers celebration-overlay with 'You filled your jar.' + reward text, then closing resets poms to 0, clears reward, and adds a shelf-jar).
critical_code_review_comments: App.js: pom-fill useEffect uses 900ms timeout to defer state resets - works but any additional completeTask clicks within 900ms after hitting 400 would enqueue more poms before reset; low risk since overlay is displayed and blocks interaction, but consider guarding with a ref.
Celebration close does not restore focus to a specific element (a11y minor).
No aria-labels on icon-only IconBtn buttons (they do have title attributes and data-testid, so functional but a11y could be improved).
seed_data_creation: Test seeded localStorage['pomjar_poms'] with 399 joy poms + reward 'Big Reward' + one trigger task to validate 400-pom celebration flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is fully client-side using localStorage keys pomjar_tasks/pomjar_drawer/pomjar_poms/pomjar_reward/pomjar_shelf. All data-testids as specified in App.js/Jar.jsx/TodayList.jsx/TaskDrawer.jsx/AddTaskForm.jsx/Celebration.jsx/Shelf.jsx are present and working. Use page.evaluate to seed 399 poms for celebration testing.
